prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the third quarter of this year of our Lord two thousand and twenty-three, the mystical entity known as "SaaS" didst beget a most bewildering parchment, whereupon it was inscribed that the "recurring revenue" hadst increased by a factor of "1.5x." Verily, I know not what manner of dark arts or magical thinking doth transpire to yield such a harvest, for there be no mention of crops, cattle, or even the gathering of taxes. The scribes didst speak of "churn rates" and "customer acquisition costs," as if they were reciting incantations to summon forth unseen riches. I didst observe many a strange ritual, where men and women stared intently at glowing rectangles, uttering phrases like "scale our enterprise solutions" and "optimize our conversion funnels," leaving mine own mind more befuddled than when I hadst begun this peculiar chronicle.
